title: Java
description: Learn Java basics — classes, types, control flow, methods, and collections on the JVM.

lessons.title: Java Introduction
lessons.subtitle: The JVM, bytecode, and your first class
lessons.theory:
Java is a statically typed, object-oriented language that runs on the Java Virtual Machine (JVM). Source code compiles to bytecode in .class files, which the JVM executes on many platforms.

Every program starts in a class with a main method: public static void main(String[] args).

The ecosystem includes JDK (development tools), JRE (runtime), and rich libraries for enterprise, Android, and backend services.

Strong typing catches many errors at compile time. Naming conventions use PascalCase for classes and camelCase for methods and variables.

definition.label: Definition
definition.text: Java is a compiled, object-oriented language whose bytecode runs on the JVM for portability and managed memory.

keyPoints: Write once, run anywhere via the JVM
main is the application entry point

codeExample.label: Hello Java

quiz.question: Where does a Java application start execution?
quiz.explanation: The JVM calls public static void main(String[] args) as the entry point.

quiz.question: Which file extension is produced after compiling Java source code?
quiz.explanation: The Java compiler produces bytecode in .class files.

quiz.question: Which component actually runs Java bytecode?
quiz.explanation: The JVM executes compiled Java bytecode.

lessons.title: Variables and Types
lessons.subtitle: Primitives, references, and declarations
lessons.theory:
Local variables need a type and name: int count = 0;. Primitive types include int, double, boolean, and char.

Reference types point to objects on the heap: String name = "Learnify";. Strings are immutable in Java.

final prevents reassignment of a variable (similar to const in other languages).

Use meaningful types instead of raw Object when possible for clarity and safety.

definition.label: Definition
definition.text: A variable is a named storage location with a fixed type that holds either a primitive value or a reference to an object.

keyPoints: Declare type before name: String s
Primitives hold values; objects use references
String is a reference type, not a primitive

codeExample.label: Variables

quiz.question: Which type is a primitive in Java?
quiz.explanation: int is a primitive; String and wrapper classes are reference types.

quiz.question: What does final do to a local variable?
quiz.explanation: final means the variable cannot be reassigned after initialization.

quiz.question: Which statement best describes a reference type?
options: It stores the object itself directly in the variable
It points to an object on the heap
quiz.explanation: Reference variables hold a reference to an object rather than the object data itself.

lessons.title: Control Flow
lessons.subtitle: if, switch, for, and while
lessons.theory:
Branch with if, else if, and else. switch works on primitives, enums, and strings (modern Java).

for loops include classic for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) and enhanced for (String s : list).

while and do-while repeat while a condition holds. do-while runs the body at least once.

Use break to exit loops and continue to skip to the next iteration.

definition.label: Definition
definition.text: Control flow statements choose which code runs and how often, using conditionals and loops with explicit braces.

codeExample.label: for loop

quiz.question: Which loop always executes its body at least once?
quiz.explanation: do-while checks the condition after running the body once.

quiz.question: Which keyword exits only the current loop?
quiz.explanation: break terminates the nearest loop or switch statement.

quiz.question: What does continue do inside a loop?
quiz.explanation: continue skips the remaining statements in the current iteration.

lessons.title: Methods
lessons.subtitle: Parameters, return types, and static methods
lessons.theory:
Methods belong to classes. They declare a return type, name, parameters, and body inside braces.

void means no return value. return exits with a value matching the declared type.

static methods belong to the class, not an instance — main is static.

Overload methods by varying parameter lists; the compiler picks the best match.

definition.label: Definition
definition.text: A method is a named block of statements on a class that can accept arguments and return a value to callers.

keyPoints: Signature includes name and parameter types
static methods call without new instances
Return type must match return statements

codeExample.label: Method

quiz.question: What keyword calls a method on the class itself without an object?
quiz.explanation: static associates a method with the class rather than instances.

quiz.question: What is method overloading?
options: Using the same method name with different parameter lists
quiz.explanation: Overloading reuses a method name with different parameters.

quiz.question: What does void mean in a method signature?
quiz.explanation: void indicates that no value is returned.

lessons.title: Collections
lessons.subtitle: ArrayList, HashMap, and generics
lessons.theory:
The java.util package provides List, Set, and Map interfaces. ArrayList is a resizable array-backed list.

Generics add type parameters: ArrayList<String> titles = new ArrayList<>();. This avoids casting and unsafe storage.

HashMap stores key-value pairs with fast lookup. Keys should implement hashCode and equals correctly.

Prefer interfaces in variable types: List<String> items = new ArrayList<>(); for flexibility.

definition.label: Definition
definition.text: Collections are framework classes that store groups of objects with well-defined operations like add, get, and remove.

keyPoints: Generics enforce element types at compile time

codeExample.label: ArrayList

quiz.question: Which collection preserves insertion order and allows duplicates?
quiz.explanation: ArrayList is an ordered List that allows duplicate elements.

quiz.question: Which interface is often preferred in variable declarations for flexibility?
quiz.explanation: Programming to the List interface makes code easier to adapt.

quiz.question: What does a HashMap store?
options: Only unique values with no keys
quiz.explanation: HashMap is used for key-value mapping with fast lookup.
